basics.name: Thomas S. Snyder
basics.label: Senior QA Engineer — Test Automation & AI-Assisted Development
basics.summary: Senior Software Test Engineer with 25+ years across Microsoft, Smartsheet, OpenMarket, and mobile/console game ecosystems. Builds automation from scratch, elevates QA practices across organizations, and reduces time-to-release through tight feedback loops with stakeholders. Integrates AI tooling — including custom MCP-based agents — into testing and development workflows.

work.name: Smartsheet
work.summary: Software Test Engineer focused on manual and automated testing of a Drupal-based website and multiple custom applications. Built Karate-based automation suite from nothing; championed automation expansion to free up engineering capacity for exploratory testing.
highlights: Built new automation suites covering 85% of all manual testing with 1,500 tests — from zero automation baseline
Maintained and expanded automation reporting for stakeholders
Used Browserstack for cross-browser and mobile testing
Managed test execution, triage, bug management, and resolution
Tracked lifecycle timelines and produced test status reports
keyResponsibilities: Drive Karate automation framework architecture, planning, and implementation
Perform manual website and exploratory testing
Manage test execution, triage, defect cycles, and lifecycle reporting
challenges: Navigating entrenched peer QA resistance to adopting automation
Customizing parallel execution, wait timings, and full-flow API testing in Karate
wins: Built Karate test suites from zero baseline to cover 85% of manual testing with 1500+ tests
Freed up QA bandwidth so developers/PMs entrusted the team with advanced exploratory testing
Expanded Karate from simple API checks to full visual UI comparison and performance coverages
lessonsLearned: Speak up louder when adding automation and introducing process/documentation improvements
Proactively investigate documentation gaps even when told everything is covered

work.name: OpenMarket
work.summary: Software Test Engineer testing web services and APIs managing SMS, MMS, and RCS traffic. Maintained legacy Fitness automation, coded new tests in Karate, and transitioned to cloud technology team testing AWS-refactored services. Principal advocate for company-wide QA standardization.
highlights: Built documentation stores for test tools and methods, standardizing QA practices across the organization
Configured hardware and software environments for manual and automated test execution
Automated tests in both Fit and Karate frameworks
Tracked lifecycle timelines and produced test status reports
keyResponsibilities: Configure machines and devices for manual and automated tests
Perform API and data-driven testing on SMS, MMS, and RCS services
Design and distribute Karate API testing templates to cloud migration teams
Document systems, test methodologies, and train developers/QA
challenges: Rapidly learning and integrating complex frameworks, containers, and CI/CD controls as a manual-heavy tester
Ramping up on Fit/Fitnesse with no prior documentation, resolving merge conflicts, and aligning peer QA practices
wins: Designed a reusable Karate API test template for cloud migration, accelerating test setup company-wide
Succeeded as a product SME by rewriting missing product/codebase documentation
Transitioned into writing long-lived, complex automated suitesDaily drove Git and CI/CD tools
lessonsLearned: Onboarding documentation updates are highly effective at exposing product and codebase gaps
Reusable templates and standardize tools greatly accelerate organizational agility

work.name: Apptio
work.summary: Software Test Engineer focused on manual testing of an IT financial management web application. Principal tester for Fortune 500 client upgrade validations — stood up instances, ran automation, administered test users.
highlights: Set up and deployed client environment instances for upgrade testing
Built product knowledge base and upcoming feature documentation
Pushed management to broaden automation capabilities
Standardized documentation and experience sharing across disciplines
keyResponsibilities: Deploy, configure, and validate Fortune 500 upgrade instances
Verify data migrations and user administration layers
Troubleshoot bespoke environment setup scripts with Dev and SDETs
challenges: Managing data migrations across highly custom, client-specific database layers
Diagnosing undocumented failures in custom migration scripts that varied between clients
wins: Cut manual regression testing cycles in half (from full day to half day) by publishing custom cheat sheets and shortcut maps
Enabled long-duration test runs to finish within a single business day
Identified and documented logical errors in development's migration scripts
lessonsLearned: Internal alignment remains challenging even when building products dedicated to IT spend communication
Rapid ramp-up and self-directed organization are critical in high-pace contract roles

work.name: Placed Inc.
work.summary: Key engineer in building the Quality Assurance department. Established baseline processes and documentation for new hires. Ran daily, weekly, and monthly test cycles for aggressive web releases and quarterly mobile releases.
highlights: Built QA department baseline processes and new-hire documentation
Drove cross-team knowledge sharing through presentations and documentation
Lead meetings and product development discussions to uphold quality standards
Ran daily, weekly, monthly test cycles for web and quarterly cycles for mobile
keyResponsibilities: Design QA baseline guidelines and onboarding handbooks
Perform manual, regression, compatibility, and security tests on Web, iOS, and Android
Test backend data ingestion pipelines producing commercial reports
Set up Jenkins triggers to signal manual test runs
challenges: Synchronizing release streams of daily web updates, quarterly app store releases, and continuous data pipeline runs
Upskilling in proxy manipulations (MitM) for negative and security testing
wins: Scaled QA department from one overworked tester to a structured team including a lead, an SDET, and manual testers
Aligned mobile app build flows in Jenkins alongside web and data deployments for true CI/CD
Delivered repeatable, robust test coverage for core data pipelines and analytics web reporting
lessonsLearned: Building a QA department requires a deep technical understanding of the product stack before laying down processes
Proxy manipulation and A/B testing tools are essential to keep up with multi-platform releases

work.name: Array Health
work.summary: Key engineer in developing the QA department for a healthcare web application. Established processes, created ramp-up documents for new hires, and promoted cross-team knowledge sharing.
highlights: Developed QA baseline processes and new-hire ramp-up documentation
Led meetings and product development discussions to enforce quality standards
Standardized documentation and experience sharing across disciplines
keyResponsibilities: Draft baseline product guides and detailed test plans
Execute manual regression passes across multiple desktop browsers
Collaborate with SDET to build Gherkin automation frameworks
Bridge communication between QA and customer support teams
challenges: Integrating complex state and federal healthcare regulatory requirements into test metrics and checklists
wins: Mapped the entire healthcare portal product stack, easing manual testing passes and paving the path for automation
Introduced QA to customer support planning, ensuring production issues directly informed QA test coverage
lessonsLearned: Involving QA early in product planning and support loops dramatically reduces defect escape rates

work.name: Microsoft
work.position: Software Test Engineer 3 — Device & App Compatibility (Windows OS)
work.summary: Subject matter expert in device and application compatibility for Windows OS. Performed weekly manual and automated build tests, developed new tests and tools as new hardware platforms were absorbed, and documented and trained testers to handle increased workloads.
highlights: Created modular testing methodology enabling rapid ramp-up of new testers across 15+ feature areas
Standardized testing processes across feature teams using shared documentation
Developed tools to automate new feature area testing as hardware platforms were added
Trained new and existing testers on tools, hardware, and processes
keyResponsibilities: Configure, deploy, and maintain device compatibility test beds
Perform manual and WTT-automated compatibility test passes
Write Batch and PowerShell scripts to scan system specs and flash OS builds
Run white-box debugging on OS core crashes and memory dumps
challenges: Provisioning and testing unstable, pre-release ARM/mobile hardware (exposing open circuits, bare boards, and raw displays)
Designing test flows that scaled across thousands of diverse consumer and enterprise devices
wins: Designed a hardware coverage matrix that allowed simultaneous testing, bypassing device queue bottlenecks
Automated firmware checks and OS flashing, saving days of prep time by executing cycles overnight
Successfully scaled automated deployment systems to provision a new compatibility lab housing hundreds of devices
Identified kernel-level failures using WinDbg stack tracing
lessonsLearned: Managing physical testing scale demands robust automation and scheduling systems (WTT)
White-box debugging tools (WinDbg, symbol maps) are essential for boundary integration issues

work.name: Microsoft
work.position: Software Test Engineer 3 — Mobile Applications (Office Mobile)
work.summary: Principal Test Engineer for mobile applications on multiple handset devices. Drove creation of test plans and cases; developed scenario-based tests for offshore team handoff after project completion.
highlights: Created the group's first scenario-based tests — enabling untrained testers to deliver professional results
Provided UI and feature feedback resulting in product stability and multi-region language improvements
Documented software defects across multiple bug tracking systems
Developed transition documentation for offshore team handoff
keyResponsibilities: Build test plans and write user experience scenario flows
Validate device compatibility and connection states for Office Mobile
Manage secure check-in/checkout device tracking systems for developers
Train offshore contract hires and document handset transition guidelines
challenges: Designing a secure device inventory tracker to support dev dogfooding and prevent loss
Formatting testing passes so offshore teams without formal handset training could deliver consistent results
wins: Completed transition and handoff to offshore team so cleanly that management was unaware of the cutover
Simplified test case design, allowing immediate execution by rotating contract hires
lessonsLearned: Visual scenario flows and user experience maps allow remote/offshore teams to execute tests with minimal oversight
Physical inventory control is vital when managing pre-release mobile devices

work.name: Hewlett-Packard
work.summary: QA engineer responsible for triage training and group reorganization. Collaborated with internal and external teams to reorganize support labs, utilized in-house tools to automate manual processes, and built machine imaging infrastructure.
highlights: Reorganized consumer and enterprise support labs to improve workflow efficiency
Built machine imaging infrastructure reducing machine recovery time
Investigated external and internal hardware bugs and configured reproduction scenarios
Researched and implemented process improvements for the department
keyResponsibilities: Reorganize support labs for workstations and server systems
Configure automated network imaging infrastructure via PXE boot
Troubleshoot hardware bugs and swap system components
Maintain Product Studio bug templates and intake triage guides
toolsUsed: Workstations, rack-mounted servers, component flashing tools
challenges: Absorbing the server lab workload single-handedly after team consolidation
Modernizing legacy setups where prior engineers ran all OS installs manually
wins: Managed both workstation and server lab workloads independently, matching team-level velocity
Configured PXE boot network setups and remote reservation systems, cutting physical lab footprints
Implemented standardized triage gates and bug forms in Product Studio to improve bug logging
lessonsLearned: Automating lab setups (PXE/WTT) is essential to scale single-engineer capacity
Understand the constraints and rules of hardware vendor-partner agreements to improve support loops

work.name: Medio Systems
work.summary: QA engineer testing ad delivery and data collection from phones. Performed plan-based and ad-hoc testing on local and remote devices to ensure delivery of ad, media, and subscription content, and validated data ingestion for analytics.
highlights: Created scenario-based tests enabling untrained testers to deliver actionable results
Validated key data ingestion paths for analytics
Collaborated with SDET team to develop API testing tools
Supported late-night deployments to live wireless devices
keyResponsibilities: Perform mobile testing using emulators and debug tools
Modify device boot flags to force staging server connections
Audit data payloads and ad-delivery packets
Validate production updates during weekly maintenance windows
challenges: Working across highly fragmented, custom debug tools for separate carriers (Verizon vs T-Mobile)
Isolating ad-delivery bugs that occurred exclusively over wireless carriers but not wired debugging cables
Executing live updates and rollbacks between 12 AM and 3 AM PST
wins: Isolated critical transmission flaws on active wireless signals that bypass emulator checks
Shipped the core ad-delivery and collection services on schedule
lessonsLearned: Formulate precise diagnostic questions to get development support on complex device setups

work.name: Microsoft
work.summary: Principal Test Engineer for the Mobile Lync application on Nokia handsets under Microsoft-Nokia partnership. Drove test plan creation and developed scenario-based tests for offshore team handoff.
highlights: Created the first scenario-based tests for the Nokia partnership enabling offshore team transfer
Managed remote offshore team progress and reporting
Provided UI and feature feedback improving stability and multi-language support
Documented defects across multiple bug tracking systems
keyResponsibilities: Flash Nokia devices using CEPC and deploy builds via serial connections
Author manual test plans and UI scenario checklists
Monitor remote offshore testing progress and compile status reports
challenges: Validating partner handset integrations under strict organizational silos with no direct developer access
Keeping offshore team members aligned and unblocked across time zones
wins: Delivered a seamless handover of Lync scenarios to the offshore team, keeping reporting quality high
Automated handset flashing and app deployments using custom serial batch scripts
lessonsLearned: Visual flowcharts and screenshots resolve international team alignment issues faster than text-heavy guides

work.name: AT&T Wireless
work.summary: Test Engineer for location-based ad delivery framework validation. Used Linux terminals for database monitoring, SOAP-based API calls, remote device farms, and device monitoring hardware.
highlights: Validated location-based ad delivery via Linux CLI, SOAP APIs, and remote device farms
Managed multi-account, multi-tiered security services for partner account management
keyResponsibilities: Simulate GPS coordinates on rack-mounted mobile device trays
Audit SOAP APIs and device logs for ad storage behaviors
Log activities in the RightNow tracking database
Manage multi-account security credentials
challenges: Verifying experimental ad-serving features amidst frequently shifting project requirements
Coping with sudden laboratory closures due to an electrical fire in the UPS backup systems
wins: Acquired specialized expertise in RF isolation testing using Faraday cages and shielding chambers
Maintained a flawless audit trail of test runs and coordinate validations in RightNow prior to project shutdown
lessonsLearned: Even major telecom entities will drop experimental systems if lab hardware is knocked out by a power failure

work.name: Microsoft
work.position: Software Test Engineer 2 — Windows Media Center (eHome)
work.summary: Test Engineer for Windows Media Center set-top boxes. Performed ad-hoc, plan-based, and automation validations for multiple set-top box devices to validate integration changes.
highlights: Configured a wide range of hardware products interfacing with the software under test
Researched new features during product lifecycle to produce targeted new tests
Managed test execution and bug tracking for set-top box integrations
keyResponsibilities: Configure AV capture cards, TV tuners, remotes, and set-top box topologies
Execute manual and automated test passes using WTT
Analyze DRM signal security and media stream decodes
Research incoming features to structure fresh test plans
challenges: Coordinating testing across isolated, hardware-siloed engineering teams
Resolving composite connection, driver, and signal issues on third-party electronics
wins: Consolidated overlapping tests into a centralized rotating matrix, cutting test runtimes and cross-training peers
Standardized DRM validation templates, preventing signal decryption bugs in production builds
lessonsLearned: Breaking down team silos through rotation matrices builds cross-skilled, faster-moving QA wings

work.name: NVIDIA
work.summary: Ad-hoc and manual testing of existing and unreleased hardware for driver releases. Ran automation test suites and tiered manual tests using experimental hardware and tools.
highlights: Tested unreleased hardware for driver releases using ad-hoc and structured manual methods
Executed automation suites alongside tiered manual test passes
keyResponsibilities: Assemble motherboard setups on open-air acrylic test benches
Rotate pre-production GPUs across benches to keep automation runs active
Run Tiers 0-2 smoke checks and Tier 3+ compatibility tests
Check signal security details inside monitor overscan spaces
challenges: Swapping delicate pre-production silicon hardware frequently without motherboard pin failures
Decoding DRM signal handshakes and driver logs embedded inside monitor overscan coordinate data
wins: Managed 4 concurrent acrylic benches and 8 graphics cards without throughput bottlenecks
Successfully verified overscan graphics data to confirm driver DRM compliance
lessonsLearned: Splitting verification into tiered blocks (0-2 for core, 3+ for edge cases) optimizes QA cycles

work.name: Microsoft
work.position: Software Test Engineer — Microsoft Game Studios
work.summary: Plan and ad-hoc based testing across a lab of varying machine configurations for a wide range of games.
highlights: Created scenario-based tests enabling untrained testers to deliver consistent results
Developed scripts to quickly configure systems after OS installation
Monitored device trends and made purchase recommendations
Documented defects and processes to aid tester transitions across tools
keyResponsibilities: Validate game performance and hardware compatibility across multiple configurations
Automate system setups using custom WMI batch scripts
Upload OS configurations and directX layers to PXE boot files
toolsUsed: PC games (Zoo Tycoon, AoE3, Vanguard, Dungeon Siege)
challenges: Manually reinstalling and configuring Windows setups for separate game iterations, creating massive waste
Locating compatibility defects across vast graphics chipsets and motherboard bounds
wins: Designed and deployed WMI-driven scripts on PXE, cutting setup times from hours to minutes
Discovered a major category of power/sleep crashes during laptop lid closures across varying GPU cards
lessonsLearned: Providing engineers creative freedom to script automation removes manual bottlenecks and uncovers complex system integration bugs

work.name: Monolith Productions
work.summary: Customer advocate delivering reproducible user issues to the QA team. Triaged incoming support tickets, reproduced issues, and delivered bug reports to dev and QA. Trained Game Master team on tools.
highlights: Created prioritization process for incoming tickets — significantly speeding up triage and creating room for more weekly patch items
Trained GMs on tools, product quirks, and player abuse patterns
Implemented agile ticket processing to eliminate stale open tickets
keyResponsibilities: Audit player emails, tickets, and forum posts using RightNow
Log into games as a GM to troubleshoot active bugs with players
File structured bug reports in Product Studio and document console commands
challenges: Recreating transient, poorly documented MMORPG bugs based on vague player writeups
Filtering ticket lines to ignore toxic or abusive player requests without slowing down queues
wins: Initiated and designed the Bug Association Representative (BAR) role to link customer support with engineering
Independently processed and triaged over 1,300 tickets a week
Documented GM debug commands, enabling the support team to clear in-game character blocks immediately
lessonsLearned: Drafting clear, transparent updates for mass client mailings prevents community backlashes during beta fixes

work.name: VMC Consulting
work.summary: End-to-end manual testing on Xbox console. Helped build, execute, and improve the test process for black-box testing. Became one of the first end-to-end 'rabbits' (dedicated completion testers) and later joined the TCR (Technical Certification Requirements) team in A/V.
highlights: Created the 'rabbit' role — dedicated tester ensuring pipeline games can be completed end-to-end
Introduced rotating shifts enabling smaller delivery windows for test passes
Process improvements led to the TCR team handling 8–16 products/games per day vs. the previous 2–4
Maintained labs, improved infrastructure, and trained new hires on testing and bug reporting
keyResponsibilities: Execute end-to-end playthroughs using exploits to test completion limits
Audit games against Xbox Technical Certification Requirements (TCR)
Monitor SDKs and devkit telemetry during AV state changes
Author manual guides and train incoming testers
toolsUsed: Original Xbox Developer Kits & debug units
challenges: Transitioning from weekly releases to a fast-moving, multi-shift 24/7 test cadence
Handling team expansions and shifting management tiers during rapid lab scaling
wins: Designed SDK and devkit training programs that tripled lab testing capacity
Pioneered the 'Rabbit' speedrun-exploit completion methodology in pre-UAT pipelines
Increased TCR testing volume from 2-4 games daily to 8-16 games daily
lessonsLearned: Mastered the foundation of manual test design, console certification parameters, and hardware telemetry

work.name: Keane Inc.
work.summary: Handled inbound tier-1 support calls for various Windows PC products.
highlights: Consistently top performer taking 16 calls per day against an 8-call service level agreement
Created boilerplate scripts standardizing the support process for new hires
Handled tier-2 and tier-3 escalations to reduce higher-tier tech workload
keyResponsibilities: Guide callers through dial-up modems and boot failure troubleshooting
Perform manual registry repairs and boot updates with users
Author support templates to standardize agent scripts
challenges: Resolving deep developer/engineer issues that bypassed normal support lines
De-escalating hostile clients to perform registry diagnostics
wins: Consistently doubled SLA requirements by processing 16 calls daily
Deflected tier-2/3 ticket loads by correcting manual flags in client registries
Achieved top monthly clean system recovery (FFR) metrics, resolving corrupted OS sessions
lessonsLearned: Built a deep understanding of Windows registries, boot sectors, and OS file setups

work.name: Nintendo of America
work.summary: Ad-hoc and plan-based manual testing on a wide range of Nintendo games for N64 and Gameboy consoles.
highlights: Implemented knowledge sharing practices enabling testers with little training to deliver professional results
Documented platform quirks enabling testers to find more hard-locks and soft-locks
Provided UI and feature feedback improving product stability and multi-language support
keyResponsibilities: Test game files on retail consoles loaded with EPROM chips
Record active gameplay feeds to analog VHS tape capture setups
Draft physical paper bug sheets and log cartridge crash points
Check audio and text assets for licensing compliance
challenges: Learning professional QA principles, coverage design, and reporting standards
Recreating transient black-box crash paths without access to terminal logs
wins: Discovered music license violations in game soundtracks, preventing invalid releases
Established standard video-capture workflows to prove complex speedrun exploits to developers
lessonsLearned: Deconstruct applications into logical categories to build deep testing structures
Use video logs as a vital aid in documenting complex reproduction sequences

education.area: Software Quality Engineering, Test Automation & AI-Assisted Development
courses: Karate DSL — BDD API and UI test automation framework
Playwright — browser automation and end-to-end testing
AI-assisted development with Antigravity, ChatGPT, Copilot, and custom MCP agents
AWS cloud services and Kubernetes orchestration
WinDbg and symbol-connected stack trace analysis

references.name: Available upon request
references.reference: Professional references available upon request. Please contact Mr.Thomas.Snyder@gmail.com.

projects.name: seikoclaw-harness
projects.description: A custom MCP-based AI agent harness and skill router built to orchestrate AI-assisted coding, testing, and development workflows. Demonstrates hands-on AI tooling and agent engineering capabilities.
highlights: Skill router for directing AI agents to specialized capabilities
Integrates with Antigravity (AGY) agentic coding assistant
Demonstrates practical AI-assisted development in a real engineering context

projects.name: TalentScout
projects.description: A resume-as-code engine with a JSON Resume compiler pipeline (JSON → Markdown → HTML → PDF), a 7-tool MCP server for AI agent interoperability, and a dynamic portfolio landing page.
highlights: Compiler pipeline with ATS adapters for Greenhouse and Workday
MCP server exposing get_basics, get_experience, get_skills, search_capabilities, and generate_ats_profile tools
29/29 automated tests passing with Vitest
Playwright-based job scraper with SQLite deduplication and FastAPI dashboard
